relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Ananias, elders, and Tertullus bring formal accusation before Felix.
Flattery opens the prosecution.
Felix is praised for peace and reform.
The advocate asks for brief hearing.
Paul is framed as pest, agitator, and Nazarene ringleader.
The temple accusation is repeated.
A later manuscript tradition adds details about Lysias.
Examination is proposed as proof.
The accusers join the charge.
Paul answers with steady defense before Felix.
He notes the short time since coming to worship.
He was not found stirring crowds in temple, synagogue, or city.
The accusations cannot be proven.
He confesses the Way as service to the ancestral Divine and trust in Law and Prophets.
His hope is resurrection of just and unjust.
He practices an untroubled conscience before Divine and humans.
He came with mercy-gifts and offerings for his nation.
He was found purified, not riotous.
The absent Asia accusers should be present if they have a case.
Let the present accusers say what wrong was found before the council.
The true issue remains resurrection of the dead.
Felix, knowing the Way more accurately, postpones judgment.
Paul is guarded with relief and access for his own.
Felix and Drusilla hear Paul concerning trust in Messiah Jesus.
Justice, self-control, and coming judgment make Felix afraid.
Felix’s curiosity is mixed with hope for money.
Two years pass, and political favor leaves Paul bound.
